original_description: Manage Claude Code plugin marketplaces — create, list, add, or remove plugins from marketplace repositories. Use when the user asks to \"create a marketplace\", \"publish a plugin\", \"list marketplace plugins\", \"add plugin to store\", \"manage plugin catalog\", \"set up a plugin registry\", \"remove from marketplace\", or anything about plugin distribution and discovery.
best_description: Manage Claude Code plugin marketplaces — create, list, add, or remove plugins from marketplace repositories. Use when the user asks to \"create a marketplace\", \"publish a plugin\", \"list marketplace plugins\", \"add plugin to store\", \"manage plugin catalog\", \"set up a plugin registry\", \"remove from marketplace\", or anything about plugin distribution and discovery.
final_description: ALWAYS invoke this skill when the user wants to create, initialize, populate, or query a plugin marketplace repository they own or manage. A marketplace is a git repo cataloging Claude Code plugins for distribution. Trigger whenever the user says "marketplace" combined with any management intent: init, create, add, publish, list, remove, set up, or configure. Examples: "init a marketplace", "add plugin to my marketplace", "publish to marketplace", "list my marketplace", "create marketplace repo", "remove from marketplace". Also trigger for "plugin store", "plugin catalog", "plugin registry" when the user is the owner/operator. Do NOT trigger for installing or browsing plugins as a consumer, releasing versions, plugin audits, or general git pushes.

history.description: Manage Claude Code plugin marketplaces — create, list, add, or remove plugins from marketplace repositories. Use when the user asks to \"create a marketplace\", \"publish a plugin\", \"list marketplace plugins\", \"add plugin to store\", \"manage plugin catalog\", \"set up a plugin registry\", \"remove from marketplace\", or anything about plugin distribution and discovery.

train_results.query: init a new marketplace at ~/dev/marketplaces/public-plugins on github
add the beads plugin to my ivintik marketplace so other machines can install it
I want to publish my new plugin to a marketplace so others can discover and install it
list all plugins in my marketplace -- I want to see whats available across both my personal and work stores
create a new marketplace repo on github called team-plugins -- private, for our internal plugin distribution
release plugin-ops 2.0.0 -- bump version, commit, push, and create a tag
install the beads plugin from the marketplace -- I saw it in the official store
optimize my plugin -- condense the knowledge files and fix broken references
push my code to the remote git repository
I want to browse the official claude plugins marketplace and see whats available
run a health audit on my plugin and tell me if the structure is correct

history.description: Use this skill when the user wants to own or operate a plugin marketplace repository. Covers: initializing/creating a new marketplace repo (on GitHub, GitLab, or locally), adding or publishing a plugin into a marketplace catalog, removing a plugin from a marketplace, listing what plugins are in a marketplace, or configuring marketplace stores. A "marketplace" here is a git repo that catalogs Claude Code plugins for distribution and discovery. Trigger for any intent around setting up, populating, or curating a plugin store/catalog/registry — including "init marketplace", "add plugin to my marketplace", "publish plugin to store", "list marketplace plugins", "create marketplace repo", "remove from marketplace". Do NOT trigger for installing/browsing plugins as a consumer, releasing plugin versions, or general plugin development tasks.

history.description: ALWAYS invoke this skill when the user mentions "marketplace" in connection with creating, managing, populating, or querying a plugin marketplace they own. Trigger phrases include: "init marketplace", "create marketplace", "add to marketplace", "publish to marketplace", "list marketplace", "remove from marketplace", "marketplace repo", "plugin store", "plugin catalog", "plugin registry". Also trigger when the user wants to set up a git repository for distributing or cataloging Claude Code plugins. Do NOT trigger for: installing/browsing plugins as a consumer, releasing plugin versions, plugin development, code pushes, or plugin audits.
